# Rates Engine

> Public REST + SSE pricing API for the Stellar network. Aggregates
> on-chain DEX/AMM trades, off-chain CEX trades, and oracle feeds
> into VWAP/TWAP/OHLC. Apache-2.0, pre-v1.

The canonical asset_id format is `<class>:<id>` — e.g.
`crypto:XLM`, `fiat:USD`, `credit:USDC:GA5ZSEJYB37JRC5AVCIA5MOP4RHTM335X2KGX3IHOJAPP5RE34K4KZVN`.
The string `native` is a shorthand for `crypto:XLM`.

## Public surfaces

- [Explorer](https://ratesengine.net): asset / market / pool / source / exchange browser, methodology docs, ADR + research browser.
- [API](https://api.ratesengine.net): REST + SSE pricing endpoints. Anonymous-friendly; magic-link sign-in unlocks higher rate tiers.
- [Docs](https://docs.ratesengine.net): OpenAPI v1 spec rendered with Scalar.
- [Status](https://status.ratesengine.net): per-region service health + incident postmortems.

## Key endpoints

- [GET /v1/price?asset=<id>&quote=fiat:USD](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/price?asset=native&quote=fiat:USD): closed-bucket VWAP for the most recent minute (cross-region byte-identical).
- [GET /v1/price/tip?asset=<id>&quote=fiat:USD](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/price/tip?asset=native&quote=fiat:USD): fastest-feed tip (lower latency, weaker consistency).
- [GET /v1/price/stream?asset=<id>&quote=fiat:USD](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/price/stream?asset=native&quote=fiat:USD): SSE stream of closed-bucket VWAPs.
- [GET /v1/markets](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/markets): every (base, quote) pair with 24h volume / trade count.
- [GET /v1/pools](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/pools): every Soroban DEX pool we observe (Soroswap, Aquarius, Phoenix, Comet, SDEX).
- [GET /v1/sources](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/sources): every venue/source we ingest from + classification (exchange / aggregator / oracle).
- [GET /v1/oracle/streams](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/oracle/streams): latest reading per (oracle, asset, quote) — Reflector / Band / Redstone / Chainlink HTTP.
- [GET /v1/coins](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/coins): activity-ranked listing with prices, sparklines, market_cap, supply.
- [GET /v1/incidents](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/incidents): active + recent SEVs with severity + affected components.
- [GET /v1/status](https://api.ratesengine.net/v1/status): service-health rollup.

## Architecture

- [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): repo orientation, invariants, and counter-intuitive facts (start here if you're an LLM agent reading the repo cold).
- [docs/architecture/ingest-pipeline.md](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/docs/architecture/ingest-pipeline.md): Galexie → dispatcher → decoder.
- [docs/architecture/aggregation-plan.md](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/docs/architecture/aggregation-plan.md): VWAP / TWAP / outlier / triangulation policy chain.
- [docs/architecture/contract-schema-evolution.md](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/docs/architecture/contract-schema-evolution.md): Soroban DeFi contracts upgrade in place; decoders must be WASM-version-aware for backfill.
- [docs/adr/](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/tree/main/docs/adr): all architecture decisions, immutable, accept-only-or-supersede.

## Data sources we ingest

- **On-chain (Stellar):** Soroswap, Aquarius, Phoenix, Comet, SDEX (via Galexie ledger archives — never via Horizon).
- **Off-chain CEX:** Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, Bitstamp.
- **Oracles:** Reflector (DEX/CEX/FX), Band, Redstone, Chainlink HTTP.
- **Aggregators (cross-check only, not VWAP-contributing):** CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, CryptoCompare.
- **Authority FX:** ECB, exchangeratesapi, polygon-forex.

## How rates are computed

- See [/methodology](https://ratesengine.net/methodology) for the full write-up.
- Short version: trades from `class=exchange` sources contribute to the VWAP. Aggregators and oracles are reported alongside but excluded from the canonical VWAP (mixing them double-counts upstream markets or imposes their methodology on top of ours).
- Stablecoin → fiat is a **late-binding** aggregator policy, not an ingest policy. We store the real pair (`XLM/USDT`, `XLM/USDC`); the aggregator maps `USDT/USDC/PYUSD → USD`, `EUROC/EUROB → EUR`, `MXNe → MXN` at VWAP compute time. Eager normalisation at ingest would hide a depeg event.

## SDK

- Go: `go get github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/pkg/client` — see [/sdk](https://ratesengine.net/sdk) for examples.

## Licensing + status

- License: Apache-2.0 ([LICENSE](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/LICENSE)).
- Status: pre-v1 (currently v0.5.0-rc.x). API surface is SemVer-stable per [docs/architecture/semver-policy.md](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/docs/architecture/semver-policy.md).
- Security disclosures: [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/SECURITY.md) — `security@ratesengine.net`.

## Optional

- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): every release notes entry.
- [/changelog.atom](https://ratesengine.net/changelog.atom): Atom feed of release notes for syndication.
- [docs/discovery/](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/tree/main/docs/discovery): Phase-1 audit archive — read-only, but referenced often for "why does Stellar / source X behave like Y".
- [docs/operations/runbooks/](https://github.com/RatesEngine/rates-engine/tree/main/docs/operations/runbooks): one runbook per alert. Read these before touching production.
